danger_signals.visual_form: Gauge displayed on bottom of screen during exploration
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates likelihood of random demon encounter; rising gauge signals imminent danger
Demons cannot be negotiated with during full moon; forces combat or flee, increasing danger

danger_signals.visual_form: First-person battle sprite and dungeon encounter graphics
danger_signals.meaning: Demon type and form communicate threat level and alignment incompatibility

danger_signals.visual_form: Post-apocalyptic world design with unbreathable air requiring city encapsulation
danger_signals.meaning: World state of catastrophic danger; survival only possible in protected Tokyo Millennium

safety_signals.visual_form: Luxurious central district architecture contrasting with outer districts
safety_signals.meaning: High-security zone ruled by religious authority; safest location for compliant citizens

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly character sprites in designated safe zones (Okamoto's gym)
safety_signals.meaning: Presence of known allies indicates safe harbor and training/preparation opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Tournament venue district with competition structure
safety_signals.meaning: Established civic institution; safety through competitive achievement and citizenship

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Religious iconography; bishop and temple knights as visual representatives
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Temple knights (Hiroko, Zayin) serve as enforcement; opposing demons appear as enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: The Center district; organized, hierarchical architecture; religious temples; controlled city zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Demon summoning visual effects; diverse demon creature designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Demons inhabit Valhalla, outer districts, and dungeons; varied creature types signal faction diversity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dungeons, outdoor wilderness areas, uncontrolled districts; post-apocalyptic ruins; areas outside Center control

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Gimmel and Arcadia forest connection; Okamoto's independent gym
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Characters like Steven operate outside main faction structures
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Arcadia forest; independent training facilities; civilian districts not directly controlled by Center

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: World environment design shows ruins, unbreathable air, need for encapsulated city Tokyo Millennium
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Humans confined to city; population pressured into survival hierarchy; gladiator tournaments as social structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Center district opulence; hierarchical architecture; religious symbols and temple knight presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs follow religious doctrine; missions assigned by bishop; citizens seek Center citizenship as reward

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Demons freely encountered in dungeons and wilderness; demon sprite designs communicate individual personality and alignment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can negotiate with demons; demon mood changes by lunar phase; some demons recruitable as allies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alignment affects NPC interactions and demon recruitment availability; plot branches reflect alignment choice
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Demons react differently based on player alignment compatibility; story outcomes diverge based on moral choices

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Beth assigned as divine partner; special treatment from Messian authority; protagonist narrative shift
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character Aleph/Hawk gains mission authority; people respond with reverence/opposition depending on alignment

environmental_storytelling.example: Valhalla gladiator district contrasts with luxurious Center, communicating social stratification and power structure
Tokyo Millennium's protective dome visually communicates post-apocalyptic catastrophe and human retreat from external world
Demons inhabit dungeons and outer districts while humans concentrate in Center; visual world-building reflects faction power distribution
Temple knights (Hiroko, Zayin), bishop, and gladiators identified by visual design; communicates faction membership without dialogue
Different dungeon visual themes reflect varying demon populations and world areas; environmental design signals exploration progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Mood-dependent; personality-driven dialogue choices; responsive to player attitude (friendly vs. intimidating); may request items/money
Combat-forced or flee-required; no dialogue option available
Escort players, issue reprimands for independent action, assign missions; hierarchical obedience expected
Acts independently despite reprimands; initiates quest to find missing boy; suggests internal faction resistance
Fights Hawk multiple times; visual repetition communicates ongoing antagonism and identity conflict
Grants Demon Summoning Program without faction coercion; enables player agency

notes: SMT2 uses first-person dungeon exploration with top-down map navigation to create dual-perspective world immersion. Moral alignment system (Law/Chaos/Neutral) is core to world-state manifestation: alignment affects NPC behavior, demon recruitment, mission outcomes, and plot branching. The Messian religious authority vs. demon-inhabited world creates inherent faction visual conflict. Lunar cycle mechanics create time-based danger variation. Amnesia protagonist (Hawk/Aleph) allows player discovery of world-state alongside character identity revelation. Demon negotiation system creates non-binary danger communication: not all demons are inherently hostile, introducing diplomatic gameplay that manifests as behavioral variance. Post-apocalyptic setting communicated entirely through environmental design and narrative exposition rather than explicit visual deterioration mechanics.
